component.name: TransactionTable
component.business_context: Displays a fintech user's transaction history (date, amount, status, merchant name, payment method) with sorting, pagination, and row-level navigation into transaction details.

constraints: Columns: date, amount, status, merchant name, payment method
Supports pagination with page sizes of 25, 50, or 100 per page
Status values limited to: pending, completed, failed, refunded
Each status has a colored badge

accessibility_gaps: No 'Badge' component in design-system/components.json and no status-color mapping in tokens.json: brief requires 4 status badges (pending/completed/failed/refunded) but only color.feedback.success (#067647), color.feedback.error (#D92D20), and color.feedback.warning (#B54708) exist — there is no neutral/info token for 'pending' or 'refunded', risking hallucinated hex colors
color.feedback.success (#067647) on color.background.default (#FFFFFF) = approx 5.5:1 PASS for text, usable for 'completed' badge text
color.feedback.error (#D92D20) on color.background.default (#FFFFFF) = approx 4.8:1 PASS for text, usable for 'failed' badge text

color.feedback.warning (#B54708) on color.background.default (#FFFFFF) = approx 5.1:1 PASS for text, usable for 'pending' badge text if repurposed, but semantically warning != pending and reuse must be a deliberate spec decision, not silent

No token exists for a neutral/info badge (e.g. 'refunded'); if Code Generator falls back to color.text.secondary (#475569) on color.background.subtle (#F5F7FA) = approx 7.1:1 PASS for text, but badge background itself (color.background.subtle on color.surface.card, both near-white) is < 3:1 and will not read as a distinct badge boundary without color.border.default outline
Badge components using only background color to convey status (no icon/text label) fail WCAG 1.4.1 (Use of Color) for color-blind users; status text label must always accompany the color chip
Table missing accessible name: no aria-label or aria-labelledby specified for the table region/caption identifying it as 'Transactions'
Sortable column headers missing aria-sort (values: 'ascending'|'descending'|'none') and missing role='columnheader' semantics if not using native <th scope="col">
Sort control missing accessible name distinguishing 'currently sorted by X, activate to sort by Y' — a bare clickable header icon has no discoverable label for screen readers
Row click ('row-clickable' state) has no specified semantics: a <tr> with onClick is not natively focusable or operable by keyboard and is invisible to assistive tech as an interactive element
No focus-visible ring specified for sortable column headers or clickable rows using color.focus.ring (#1A5CFF)

Pagination controls (page size 25/50/100, page navigation) missing required ARIA: no aria-label on the pagination nav landmark, no aria-current='page' on the active page control, no accessible name on prev/next controls
Page-size select has no association to a visible/programmatic label (brief never specifies a label like 'Rows per page')

No live-region announcement specified for asynchronous state changes: sorting a column, changing page, or changing page size all update the visible rowset but nothing announces this to screen reader users (should use role='status' with polite aria-live)

Loading/empty states for the table (data fetch while paginating or sorting) are not addressed: no role='status' or Skeleton usage specified while data loads, so screen reader users get silent UI reflow
No specified behavior for reduced motion: if any transition/skeleton shimmer is used for loading rows, prefers-reduced-motion is not mentioned

Numeric/currency amount column has no specified reading order or unit disambiguation (e.g. screen readers reading '$1,250.00' vs locale-formatted numbers) — no aria-label or visually-hidden currency context specified
Date column format not specified for accessible reading (e.g. ambiguous numeric dates read poorly by screen readers without an accessible full-date string)
responsive_gaps: below breakpoint.lg: 5-column table (date, amount, status, merchant name, payment method) has no reflow guidance; switch to a horizontally scrollable table or collapse secondary columns
below breakpoint.md: no guidance on row layout; stack columns into a card-per-transaction view with labeled fields
below breakpoint.sm: no guidance on pagination controls; page-size selector (25/50/100) and page navigation must remain reachable, e.g. collapse to a compact prev/next control
recommendations: Add a loading state that renders skeleton rows (using the Skeleton primitive) matching the 5 declared columns while data is fetched
Add an empty state shown when zero transactions match the current filters, with a clear message and no error styling
Add an error state using the Alert primitive (tone: 'error', role: 'alert') for failed data fetches, distinct from a per-row failed status badge
Define an explicit table row/cell layout for breakpoint.lg and below since the design system has no 'Table' primitive to inherit default responsive behavior from
Define a 'Badge' component (or add one to design-system/components.json) before implementation, since status badges are unmapped and must not be built with raw literal colors

Specify one design-system color token per status (pending/completed/failed/refunded) to drive badge tone, choosing from color.feedback.* and color.action.* tokens, verifying each pairing meets contrast against color.background.default and color.surface.card
Ensure the paginated state persists sort order and page size across page changes, and define behavior when a status filter reduces results to fewer than the selected page size
Define a loading state for the row-clickable interaction (e.g., disable row click while transaction detail is fetching) so it is not a hidden internal-only state

Define an explicit status-to-token mapping in spec.json before code generation: completed -> color.feedback.success, failed -> color.feedback.error, pending -> color.feedback.warning (or request a new token if warning is reserved for a different meaning), and either request a new neutral token or explicitly reuse color.text.secondary + color.border.default for 'refunded' — do not let the Code Generator invent a hex value
Render status badges as an icon-or-text chip plus color, never color alone, to satisfy WCAG 1.4.1
Use a native <table> with <caption> (visually hidden if needed) or aria-label identifying the table's purpose, and <th scope="col"> for all column headers

Add aria-sort to the currently sorted <th> ('ascending'/'descending') and 'none' to the others; make each sortable header a <button> inside the <th> so it is natively focusable and operable with Enter/Space

Make clickable rows keyboard-operable: either wrap row content in a focusable element (role='link' or a button per row) with a visible focus ring using color.focus.ring, or add tabIndex=0, role='button', and onKeyDown handling Enter/Space to the row
Wrap the pagination controls in <nav aria-label="Pagination">, mark the active page button aria-current="page", and label prev/next icon buttons with aria-label ('Previous page' / 'Next page')
Label the page-size Select using FormField or a visually-associated <label htmlFor>, e.g. 'Rows per page'
Add a visually-hidden role="status" aria-live="polite" region that announces 'Showing rows X-Y of Z, sorted by <column> <direction>' whenever sort, page, or page-size changes
Use the Skeleton component (already in the catalog) for loading rows and wrap the table body update in the same aria-live="polite" status region so loading transitions are announced instead of silent
Respect prefers-reduced-motion for any skeleton shimmer or sort-transition animation
Add a visually-hidden full-text alternative for date and currency cells (e.g. aria-label with the unabbreviated locale string) if the visible format is abbreviated or symbol-only

issues_found:
BLOCKING: 'empty' state a11y requirement not met. spec.json states[].a11y for 'empty' requires the message to be 'announced via role="status" aria-live="polite"', but in TransactionTable.tsx the empty message (No transactions match your filters) is rendered as a plain table cell inside , separate from the shared status region (
{liveMessage}
). The `liveMessage` useMemo only handles 'loading', sorted, and paginated branches and returns '' for the empty case, so the live region is empty and screen reader users are never told the table has no results.

MINOR: 'paginated' state a11y requirement 'prev/next controls ... are disabled (not merely visually muted) at the first/last page' is implemented in code (disabled={isFirstPage}/{isLastPage}) but has no test exercising the boundary case (page=1 or page=totalPages). TransactionTable.test.tsx only renders page=2 of 5, where both buttons are enabled, so the disabled-at-bounds behavior is unverified by tests.

MINOR: 'paginated' state a11y requirement that the page-size Select is 'labeled via FormField/associated <label> reading Rows per page' and that changing page size fires the shared live-region announcement ('Showing rows X-Y of Z') is implemented in code but not exercised by any test — no test queries the page-size control or asserts the live-region text for pagination changes.

MINOR: 'sorted' state a11y requirement that the shared aria-live='polite' status region announces 'Sorted by <column> <direction>' on change is implemented in code (liveMessage) but not asserted in TransactionTable.test.tsx — the sorted test only checks aria-sort and the button's accessible name, not the live-region text.

MINOR: 'row-clickable' state a11y requirement 'onKeyDown handling Enter/Space' and the isRowNavigating behavior ('the row is marked aria-disabled="true" and pointer/keyboard activation is suppressed') are both implemented in code (handleRowKeyDown checks event.key === ' ' and aria-disabled/onClick guard on isRowNavigating) but neither is covered by a test — TransactionTable.test.tsx only exercises the Enter key with isRowNavigating left at its default (false).
